title: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
summary: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.

title: Trump Reimposes a Naval Blockade of Iran
summary: Plus, Lindsey Graham’s sister was appointed to his Senate seat. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

title: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
summary: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.

title: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Appointed to Finish His Senate Term After Trump’s Backing
summary: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina named Darline Graham Nordone to replace Mr. Graham, who died on Saturday.

title: How Lindsey Graham’s Journey From Trump Critic to Ally Made Him a Power Player
summary: He initially called Donald Trump a “kook” who was “unfit for office,” only to join the new president’s Mar-a-Lago circle. “I’m still in the game,” Mr. Graham once explained of his much-analyzed turnabout.

title: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
summary: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.

title: Judge Denounces Trump’s I.R.S. Suit as Improper Exercise in Self-Dealing
summary: The decision also recommended disciplinary action against the lawyers involved, including the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche.

title: Trump Sharply Cuts the Size of Two National Monuments in Utah
summary: Native American tribes and environmental groups are expected to challenge the move to shrink the Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monuments.

title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
summary: Some tech executives are nervous that the administration’s recent scrutiny of artificial intelligence models could be a prelude to a demand for an ownership stake.

title: Trump Administration Announces New Attack on the International Criminal Court
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the United States will “dismantle” the international court that tries grave crimes “brick by brick, if necessary.”

title: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Chased the American Dream for 35 Years Before Fatal ICE Shooting
summary: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo hoped to obtain legal status, his family said. He was killed last week by an immigration agent who was looking for a different man.

title: ICE Shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Through Open Passenger Window in Houston, Witnesses Say
summary: A congresswoman and a lawyer relayed witness accounts that diverge from Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s version of the fatal encounter.

title: Democratic Senate Candidates in Maine Denounce Deadly ICE Shooting
summary: The killing of a man in Biddeford quickly became an issue in the Senate race, with Democrats aiming to tie Senator Susan Collins to President Trump’s immigration crackdown.

title: ICE Agent Kills Person in Vehicle in Biddeford, Maine, State Officials Say
summary: The fatal shooting in Biddeford, Maine, was the second in a week involving an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent firing into a vehicle.

title: How a Teacher Revived Backyard Baseball
summary: Backyard Baseball, a favorite game for 1990s kids, had been off the market for years. But Lindsay Barnett was determined.

title: The Next Phase in Ukraine’s War With Russia: The Battle for Minds
summary: Ukrainians, already drone innovators, are turning their focus to psychological operations aimed at undercutting Russian support for the war.

title: Health Officials in Michigan Identify ‘Potential Source’ of Cyclospora Outbreak
summary: The illness, which has sickened thousands across much of the United States, is on track to contribute to the largest outbreak in recent history.

title: This California Oil Pipeline Was Dormant for 11 Years. Trump Reopened It.
summary: The move in Santa Barbara by the Trump administration has made residents anxious in a region prized for its gorgeous beaches and ocean views.

title: Housing or Flood Protection? In Brooklyn, Housing Is Winning.
summary: A towering apartment development has been approved for the waterfront, even though some residents would have preferred parkland.

title: The eBay Scandal Gets a Thriller-Like Documentary
summary: A newly available documentary, ‘Whatever It Takes,’ examines eBay workers’ plot to cyberstalk journalists and critics.

title: In Turning to Trade School, Gen Z Confronts an Enduring Stigma
summary: Gen Z is increasingly turning to trade schools in hopes of future-proofing their careers against A.I. But getting their parents and peers on board can be a challenge.

title: The Ridiculous Rise of Victor Marx
summary: Once started, the process of voter radicalization isn’t easy to modulate.

title: Bison Attacks Tourist at Yellowstone National Park, Tossing Him Into the Air
summary: A man was hospitalized after a visibly agitated bull bison flipped him about eight feet in the air near a campground road.
